text_content: If at any time the measurements derived from the two ADCs do not lie within the required tolerance, a fault is asserted that stops the beam. Primary and secondary monitoring is required to ensure that safety is not compromised by erroneous reporting of a monitoring system. Dose computed by the two monitoring systems is compared against the commanded or ordered dose for every pulse. If the dose reported by either monitoring systems deviates beyond the control limits, a fault shall be asserted that will stop the beam. These two monitoring systems share the following common elements: • Low Voltage Power Supply • Single communication path to the supervisor SPV IEC 60601-2-1 Edition 4.0 2020 D65872 - Test Report for 201.10.101.1.2 DOSE MONITORING SYSTEMS This document contains confidential, proprietary information of Varian Medical Systems. The adaptor should be able to detect any equipment deviations or malfunctions, understand safe operating limits of the equipment and should be able to judge when, due to equipment malfunctions and problems, or errors in the proposed treatment, to withhold or terminate treatment until the problem is resolved. Duties of this actor may be regulated by local regulations and laws.
